frameworks.name: decoded neurofeedback (DecNef)
frameworks.one_line: reward-pair decoded fMRI representations of feared animals without conscious exposure

frameworks.name: hyperalignment
frameworks.one_line: infer target representations from surrogate participants to bypass conscious encounter

frameworks.name: associative reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: monetary reward paired with covert neural states to reprogram fear responses

frameworks.name: exposure-based fear extinction
frameworks.one_line: conventional conscious exposure therapy, here bypassed to reduce aversion-driven dropout

core_question: Can physiological fear responses to naturally feared animals be reduced by pairing reward with unconscious occurrences of their decoded neural representations?
key_assumptions: fMRI multivariate patterns can index category-specific fear representations
hyperalignment transfers representational geometry across participants
unconscious reinforcement of a neural state can alter the downstream physiological response it indexes
manipulations: pairing of monetary reward with decoded feared-animal representations vs control
use of surrogate-derived (hyperaligned) decoder rather than participant's own conscious exposure
key_findings: reliable reductions in skin conductance responses to feared animals
reductions in amygdala hemodynamic activity to feared animals
effects obtained without conscious encounter with the feared category
interpretations: naturally occurring fear responses can be reprogrammed outside conscious awareness
enables rigorous double-blind, placebo-controlled testing of a psychological intervention
